summary.notes: No cleanliness issues introduced by this diff. Both changed files are structurally valid, correctly named, in correct locations, and contain no temp/orphan/misplaced content. Full-repo mandatory scripts (detect-orphan-agents, detect-orphan-scripts, detect-orphan-commands, detect-dead-functions, detect-duplicate-content) found no issues attributable to the changed files; their full-repo findings are pre-existing and advisory-only per default-safe rule (non-diff-aware mode for those checks).

manifest_json.location: correct — tests/generated/<task_id>/ is the designated output path for test-writer generated artifacts
manifest_json.naming: correct — follows lower naming convention documented in tests/generated/README.md

dev_report.location: correct — docs/dev/ is the designated path for dev cycle artifacts
dev_report.naming: correct — follows kebab naming convention: dev-report-{task_id}.json pattern consistent with sibling files

inspection_detail.gitignore_note: tests/generated/ is intentionally tracked per test-writer contract. The .gitignore documents this at line 92 with an explicit comment. The session-gitignore-propagate.sh hook uses grep -qF substring matching — the comment line containing 'tests/generated/' satisfies the grep check, so the hook will not append a duplicate active rule. No gitignore coverage gap exists for this diff.

detect_orphan_agents.scope_note: Pre-existing finding — not in --changed-files scope. Advisory only, non-blocking.

detect_orphan_scripts.scope_note: Pre-existing findings — not in --changed-files scope. Advisory only, non-blocking.
detect_orphan_scripts.summary: Script produced multiple findings for scripts/todo/ and other scripts; all pre-existing, none related to changed files.

detect_duplicate_content.scope_note: Pre-existing findings in projects/, todos/, worktrees/ directories — not related to changed files. Advisory only, non-blocking.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 3 issues with the draft findings: (1) FALSE_POSITIVE — the gitignore_coverage finding is incorrect because .gitignore intentionally tracks tests/generated/ per test-writer contract; (2) UNSAFE_RECOMMENDATION — recommending 'add tests/generated/ as active gitignore rule' would break the test-writer contract by hiding version-controlled pytest artifacts; (3) MISLEADING_MECHANISM — the draft incorrectly claimed grep -F finds no active line, but the hook uses substring matching and the comment line satisfies the check.

findings.issue: FALSE_POSITIVE — gitignore_coverage finding should not be emitted. .gitignore explicitly documents tests/generated/ as intentionally tracked, and the changed manifest/test artifacts are functional tracked test-writer outputs.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace gitignore_coverage: [ ... ] with gitignore_coverage: [] and update summary to 0 findings.
findings.rationale: Codex is correct. The .gitignore comment at line 92 is an intentional exception that documents the tracking contract. The session hook grep -qF matches the comment substring so no active rule would be appended. Emitting a false major finding would incorrectly signal a cleanliness issue where none exists.

findings.issue: UNSAFE_RECOMMENDATION — recommending 'Add tests/generated/ as active gitignore rule' would hide future generated pytest/manifests that this repo intentionally version-controls.
findings.rationale: Adding tests/generated/ as an active ignore rule would contradict the test-writer contract that requires version-controlling per-cycle pytest files. The recommendation was removed.

findings.issue: MISLEADING_MECHANISM — draft claimed grep -F finds no active line, but the hook's grep check uses substring containment and the comment line contains tests/generated/, so the hook would not fire.
findings.proposed_fix: State that .gitignore contains an intentional tracked exception, not a missing rule.
findings.rationale: The hook implementation uses grep -qF which matches substrings. Line 92 of .gitignore contains the literal string 'tests/generated/' inside a comment. This satisfies the grep check. The gitignore_coverage finding was built on a false premise.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All three Codex findings were applied: gitignore_coverage array cleared, unsafe recommendation removed, gitignore_note in inspection_detail updated to accurately reflect the intentional tracking contract and hook behavior.
